summary: Russian forces are using the NRK Gnome-ND drone carrier to launch FPV drones remotely for engineering and logistical tasks. In Donetsk region, a Ukrainian FPV drone struck a Russian occupier’s bike, while in Mykolaiv region, a Russian FPV drone hit an ambulance, wounding the driver and a doctor. Russian forces downed a Ukrainian FPV drone using an FPV interceptor. The ZALA Z-16 UAV guided FAB-500 bombs to destroy a bridge in Dnipropetrovsk region, and ZALA Lancet loitering munitions struck multiple enemy targets despite EW interference. In Novorossiysk Bay, a Ukrainian drone attack hit the Russian frigate "Admiral Essen," detonating PK-10 grenade launchers and damaging the TK-25 EW complex. Ukrainian company Vyriy is developing new "Lightning" UAVs with retargeting capabilities. The Russian "Rubicon" Center intercepted over 1,500 Ukrainian fixed-wing UAVs, downing more than 70 models, with 75% belonging to 11 specific types. On March 6-7, 2026, Rubicon operators destroyed 107 and 88 targets respectively across multiple directions. Ukrainian forces report a shortage of Russian targets within FPV drone range, suggesting a decline in enemy troop density.
